div.head: The Ambassador in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 636. Department’s 222, September 8, 6 p.m. Reactions of Embassies to proposed international train from Hankow to Canton are as follows: British generally against evacuation, Hankow Consul General has full powers. French have practically no nationals wishing to evacuate and are not interested. Italian and German will cooperate.
2. My 607 [626?], September 8, 6 p.m.,Not printed. was addressed to Hankow for action toward obtaining definite information regarding number of American citizens willing to evacuate by train.
Department’s September 8, 6 p.m., and this reply have been repeated to Hankow, Peiping and Shanghai.
